signature.plsql: function create_invoice(p_customer_id in number, p_amount in number, p_description in varchar2 := null, p_vat_code in varchar2 := null) return number
signature.java: public Long createInvoice(Long customerId, BigDecimal amount, String description, String vatCode) throws ApplicationException

validations.plsql_code: appl_error_pkg.assert (p_customer_id is not null, 'Customer ID must be specified!');
validations.plsql_logic: assert(condition, error_message) => if NOT condition then raise_application_error(-20000, error_message)
validations.java_translation: if (customerId == null || customerId <= 0) { throw new ApplicationException("Customer ID must be specified!"); }
validations.verification: Ensure customerId is not null and not <= 0

validations.plsql_code: appl_error_pkg.assert (p_amount is not null, 'Amount must be specified!');
validations.plsql_logic: Null check - amount parameter cannot be null
validations.java_translation: if (amount == null) { throw new ApplicationException("Amount must be specified!"); }

validations.plsql_code: appl_error_pkg.assert (p_amount > 0, 'Amount must be greater than zero!');
validations.plsql_logic: Strict positive check - amount must be greater than (not equal to) zero
validations.java_translation: if (amount.compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) <= 0) { throw new ApplicationException("Amount must be greater than zero!"); }
validations.error_message: Amount must be greater than zero!

validations.plsql_code:
l_customer := customer_pkg.get_customer (p_customer_id);
appl_error_pkg.assert (l_customer.customer_id is not null, 'Customer not found!');
validations.sql_operation: SELECT * FROM xy_customer WHERE customer_id = p_customer_id
validations.exception_handling: exception when no_data_found then l_returnvalue := null; (in called procedure)
validations.plsql_logic: Fetch customer record and check if exists
validations.java_translation:
Customer customer = customerRepository.findById(customerId).orElse(null);
if (customer == null) { throw new ApplicationException("Customer not found!"); }
validations.verification: Ensure customer exists in database before proceeding

validations.plsql_logic: String equality comparison - must match constant exactly
validations.java_translation: if (!customer.getCustomerStatus().equals("ACTIVE")) { throw new ApplicationException("Customer not active!"); }

validations.plsql_code: appl_error_pkg.assert (l_returnvalue is not null, 'Failed to create invoice!');
validations.plsql_logic: Verify that INSERT operation returned a valid invoice_id
validations.java_translation: if (invoiceId == null || invoiceId <= 0) { throw new ApplicationException("Failed to create invoice!"); }
validations.verification: Ensure database insert was successful and returned valid ID

steps.description: System default description if user didn't provide one

calculations.java_translation: String description = p_description != null ? p_description : (getDefaultInvoiceDescription("STANDARD") != null ? getDefaultInvoiceDescription("STANDARD") : "Untitled");
calculations.verification: Ensure three-level fallback logic is preserved

calculations.plsql_logic: Conditional if-then-else with function call and null coalescing

true_branch.description: Calculate VAT, default to 0 if null

false_branch.description: No VAT code means zero VAT

calculations.verification: Ensure conditional logic is exactly replicated with proper null handling

sql_operations.verification: SELECT should return single row or null

sql_operations.underlying_sql: SELECT nvl(vat_rate, 0) FROM xy_vat WHERE vat_code = :vatCode
sql_operations.verification: VAT calculation must use correct formula and handle null rates

sql_operations.underlying_sql:
INSERT INTO xy_invoice (invoice_amount, vat_amount, invoice_description, invoice_status)
VALUES (:amount, :vatAmount, COALESCE(:description, 'Standard Invoice'), 'DRAFT')
RETURNING invoice_id INTO :invoiceId
